ATTACHΜΕΝΤ .
A party may , at or after the commencement of an action , have an
attachment against the property of the defendant, and may include
garnishees , that is any person holding property or money , belonging
to the defendant , as a security for the satisfaction of any judgment
he may get against such defendant . An attachment can be secured
against the following persons under the cirmcumstances given .
(a) ( 1) Against a defendant who is a foreign corporation, or
non -resident of the state. ( 2) One who has been absent from the
state for four months . ( 3) One who has departed from the state
intending to defraud his creditors . (4) One who has left the county
of his residence to avoid service of summons . ( 5) One who conceals
himself to prevent the service of summons . ( 6) One who has removed
or is about to remove his property or a material part of same from
the state, not leaving enough to satisfy plaintiff's claim . (7) One
who has sold , conveyed or otherwise disposed of his property , or
suffered or permitted same to be sold, with fraudulent intent to cheat ,
hinder or delay his creditors . (8) One who is about to sell or dis-
pose of his property with such intent .
(b) In an action for the recovery of money due on a contract ,
judgment or award, if the defendant have no property in the state
subject to execution , or not enough to satisfy the demand , and the
collection of the claim , will be delayed in obtaining judgment and
finding of property , an attachment may issue , as well as in an action
to recover personal property which has been ordered delivered to
plaintiff , and which property or a part of it, has been disposed of,
concealed , or removed so that the order of delivery can not be exe-
cuted .
HOW OBTAINED - When the petition or claim has been filed,
the clerk of the court , in the cases above mentioned , may issue an
attachment if the affidavit of the plaintiff show , ( 1) the nature of
the plaintiff's claim , ( 2) the sum which plaintiff believes he ought
to recover , (3) that the claim is just , and (4) the existence of any of
the grounds above named . If an officer returns a summons showing
that the defendant has left the county, or so concealed himself for
the purpose of avoiding service of summons , it will be equivalent to
the statement of the same fact in the affidavit . No attachment can
be issued until the plaintiff has executed bond , with good security ,
for double the amount of his claim , to the effect that the defendant
shall be paid any damages he may suffer if the attachment was
wrongfully obtained . The order of attachment is directed to the
sheriff or other officer , whose duty it is to take the property and
hold same until the rights of the parties may be determined in the
court. The officer levying the attachment , if he has any doubt as to
whether the property is subject to attachment , may require
